items.title: Where to now for one of WA's famous rivers?

children.content: Public submissions for the million-dollar draft Fitzroy-Derby water management plan are closing, with significant work ahead to balance environmental, pastoral and traditional owner interests.

items.title: Bronze tugboat propellers worth $2m stolen in WA's north

image.altText: Bronze propellers in yellow frames in an industrial yard.

children.content: In a heist not seen before in this WA mining town, thieves have allegedly made away with eight tonnes of tugboat propellers, with the copper estimated to be worth an eye-watering $2 million.

items.title: 'Terrible tragedy': Minister won't request coronial inquest into baby's death

children.content: WA's health minister says she would support a coronial inquest into the death of an eight-month-old girl at Kalgoorlie Health Campus, but says it is a decision for the state coroner.

articles.title: Family still reeling after death of baby in regional WA

children.content: The parents of Willow Horne are still looking for answers after the eight-month-old died in a regional WA hospital.

image.altText: A baby with hands on top of balancing ball

items.title: Woman found safe after five-day search in country WA

children.content: The desperate search for a 46-year-old woman who disappeared in southern Western Australia last week ends with her being found safe and well.

image.altText: A woman smiling at camera with grass in background

items.title: Sam Kerr returns to the United States after Chelsea exit

children.content: Matildas striker Sam Kerr has returned to the United States following her successful stint at London giants, Chelsea.

image.altText: Sam Kerr high fives Chelsea teammates Ellie Carpenter and Keira Walsh.

items.title: Court shutdown to end after Department of Justice resolves security dispute

children.content: Regional West Australian courts closed amid a stoush over security services are set to reopen about a month earlier than originally planned.

Exterior shot of the Fitzroy Crossing courthouse in bright sunshine.

items.title: Treasurer 'willing to engage' on super for under-18s

children.content: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says he's open to discussing a guarantee for superannuation to be paid for teenage workers but stresses that's not the government's focus at the moment. Follow live.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit standing in front of an Australian flag.

items.title: 'We don't fully understand': The footy risk few parents grasp

children.content: Contact is an essential part of footy, but there is growing evidence it can also be a grave threat to the health and welfare of its players. Experts say the AFL should be acting now to protect current and future players.

image.altText: Players compete for the ball at a junior Australian Rules football match.

avatar.alt: Portrait of ABC journalist Jessica Halloran.

items.title: New fellowship for mid-career journalists honours Judith Whelan

children.content: The ABC, The Sydney Morning Herald and Sydney University have created a new fellowship to honour the late Judith Whelan, a trailblazing leader in media.

image.altText: A blonde woman smiling with black round glasses.

items.title: Social media giants face fines for failing to hand over documents

children.content: Labor wants to double the fines for tech giants who refuse to hand over documents that could prove they are failing to enforce Australia’s social media age ban.

image.altText: A woman in an burnt orange suit, gestures with her hands while standing at a lectern.

items.title: More Australians are doing their own tax returns and making these mistakes

children.content: With side hustles, working from home, crypto investments, renting out rooms and receiving income through digital platforms or apps, experts say people are at greater risk of making errors with their tax.

gallery.altText: woman looking at laptop on the couch with paperwork

thumbnail.altText: woman looking at laptop on the couch with paperwork

avatar.alt: Kellie Scott black and white headshot

items.title: Hastie talks tough on One Nation while Taylor hesitates

children.content: As Angus Taylor grapples with the Coalition's dilemma of how to keep the centre while diverting voters away from Pauline Hanson's party, Andrew Hastie lays out his vision for fighting One Nation.

image.altText: Andrew Hastie stands in a crowd of people wearing a suit and tie with a sticker showing his name.

items.title: Report finds 'extensive' coal and gas presence in schools and children's sport

children.content: Santos, BHP, Woodside and others have an "extensive presence" in childhood settings through more than 260 programs, a new report identifies, as senators say classroom programs and lessons written by the industry should be examined through a senate inquiry.

image.altText: Children look at a laptop. The word Glencore can be seen on the lid. A person in an orange high-vis shirt is nearby.

items.title: Will you raise the white flag on these tricky flag questions?

children.content: How many stars are there on the US flag? And how many stars are on the Europe flag?

image.altText: The Australian, Aboriginal and Torres Strait flags fly in Canberra.

items.title: NDIS shell company boom sparks crackdown

children.content: The federal government says it is moving to crack down on the growing trade in "off-the-shelf" NDIS providers.

image.altText: A middle aged white woman with short fair hair and red suit sitting in an office chair

items.title: Woman attacked by pack of dogs says owners did nothing to help

children.content: A woman who says she was attacked by a pack of dogs that were being walked off-leash in Western Australia's far north is calling for more responsible pet ownership.

gallery.altText: Street sign with long road with car approaching bend in background

thumbnail.altText: Street sign with long road with car approaching bend in background

items.title: ASX trading sideways, Wall Street jumps as AI-related stocks rebound

children.content: The Australian share market has started its day relatively flat despite a rebound in oil prices ahead of the next round of US-Iran peace talks. On Wall Street, the Dow Jones Industrial Average hit a record high. Follow live.

image.altText: Picture of stock market board with ASX sign at the top left corner.

items.title: Popular 'rewards club' banned in NSW as national crackdown looms

children.content: One of Australia's biggest "rewards clubs" has been banned from running any gaming activity in New South Wales for 18 months after allegedly breaking the law.

image.altText: A luxurious house on the gold coast with a large pool, surrounded by palm trees.

items.title: The medicine safety system most Australians have never heard of

children.content: A little black triangle on medicine information leaflets is meant to make it easier to report adverse effects of new medications. Problem is, many Australians don't know what it means.

image.altText: A pile of drugs on a table, all are different shapes and sizes.

items.title: Bondi terror attack survivor received 'influx of hate' hours after shooting

children.content: Deepfake and altered images depicting a Bondi Beach terror attack survivor holding an Academy Award while covered in blood spread "like wildfire" online just hours after the shooting, a royal commission has heard.

image.altText: Arsen Ostrovsky in a suit and blue tie outside a building

items.title: Disruption-plagued Perth-to-Bunbury train service resumes after years of delays

children.content: The disruption-plagued Perth-to-Bunbury train service is back on track following years of breakdowns and delays, but locals remain frustrated over the rollout.

items.title: ABC presenter Trevor Chappell to hang up mic after 26 years

children.content: After decades of keeping night owls and shift workers company on ABC radio across Australia, Overnights presenter Trevor Chappell decides to call it a day at the age of 65.

image.altText: Overnights presenter Trevor Chappell wears a blue shirt and stands near the arts centre

items.title: Tourism operator says 'silence is deafening' about how to spot bird flu cases

children.content: A tourism operator on South Australia's coast is frustrated by what he says is poor communication from authorities about bird flu. He says he saw sick birds weeks ago but did not know to report the sighting.

image.altText: A giant petrel bird hides in green seaside foliage at Knights Beach on the Fleurieu Peninsula.

items.title: Osaka channels Kill Bill as Sinner and Sabalenka reverse French misery

children.content: Jannik Sinner is pushed to the brink and Aryna Sabalenka coasts into the second round as both top seeds bounce back from disappointment at the French Open.

image.altText: Naomi Osaka warms up in her kimono at Wimbledon.

items.title: Former England captain says Brook can't lead Test side under McCullum

children.content: Ben Stokes says vice-captain Harry Brook has his "100 per cent" support to lead England's Test side, but coach Brendon McCullum is less effusive.

image.altText: Ben Stokes with his arm around Harry Brook during the national anthem.

items.title: Key observatory shut for months prior to bird flu detection

children.content: Volunteers and wildlife advocates push to reopen the Eyre Bird Observatory, arguing the station will be critical in monitoring the potential impact of avian flu.
